description_long: This object consists of a group of six irregularly shaped stones, each with unique contours and surface textures. The stones vary in size and color, ranging from light gray to dark gray, with some showing hints of brown or beige. Their surfaces are mostly rough and matte, with visible natural veining and subtle pitting, suggesting they have been weathered over time. The stones appear to be loosely grouped, not physically connected, and each has a distinct, organic form typical of naturally occurring rocks. Some stones have smoother, rounded edges, while others display sharper, more angular facets. These stones would commonly be found scattered or arranged as a low barrier or decorative feature in urban parks, gardens, or along pathways.
description: A loosely grouped set of six irregular, weathered stones in varying shades of gray and brown, each with unique natural textures and shapes.
description_view_0: Six stones are visible in a horizontal row, showing their varied sizes, colors, and surface textures.
description_view_1: The stones are seen edge-on, revealing their thin profiles and irregular thicknesses.
description_view_2: The stones are viewed from the opposite side, displaying the reverse surfaces and highlighting different contours and color patterns.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, showing the stones' profiles and how they are spaced apart.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loosely grouped along the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural veining and mottling with irregular, organic surface patterns; no artificial carvings or regularity.
attribute_weathering: Surfaces are rough and matte, with visible pitting and rounded edges indicating moderate weathering from outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or landscaped greenery, often partially embedded or surrounded by low vegetation.
